{"step_by_step_thinking": "Option A states that all intrinsic muscles of the larynx are supplied by the recurrent laryngeal nerve. This is true as the recurrent laryngeal nerve supplies all intrinsic muscles of the larynx except the cricothyroid muscle, which is supplied by the external laryngeal nerve. Option B states that the cricothyroid muscle is supplied by the external laryngeal nerve, which is correct. Option C states that the posterior cricoarytenoid muscle abducts the vocal cords, which is true. Option D states that the lymphatic drainage of the larynx is to the deep cervical nodes, which is not entirely accurate as the lymphatic drainage is divided into supraglottic and infraglottic regions, with different nodes receiving the lymph. Therefore, option D is not true.", "answer_choice": "D"}
